title: "Ivory Phoenix Enigma v10313"

description: "Within the midnight halls of the Imperial Aerie, an artifact named the Ivory Phoenix vanished exactly at 23:50. Four individuals (1, 2, 3, 4) had exclusive clearance at that late hour. Exactly one suspect lies in every statement they present, and only one suspect is actually guilty; the remaining three speak pure truth. Each suspect utters five claims tied to times and either-or statements:

  Suspect 1:
    (1) 'If 2 is lying, then 3 was in no position to steal the Phoenix.'
    (2) 'I exited before 23:40, so I was far from the artifact when it disappeared.'
    (3) 'Either 2 lingered past 23:35, or 4 has falsified everything—but never both.'
    (4) 'If 3 is honest, then 2's statement about departure must be nonsense.'
    (5) 'I never held that Ivory Phoenix after 23:30.'

  Suspect 2:
    (1) '1 actually stayed until 23:45, which contradicts the “23:40 exit” story.'
    (2) 'If 3 is lying, then 4’s timeline is completely valid.'
    (3) 'I was nowhere near that display after 23:25.'
    (4) 'Either 1’s departure claim is untrue, or 3 is guilty—but not both.'
    (5) 'If 4 is not involved, then 1’s second statement must be a lie.'

  Suspect 3:
    (1) 'I did not approach the Phoenix after 23:20.'
    (2) 'If 4's story is genuine, then 2’s claim (3) is unsound.'
    (3) 'Either 2 is lying, or 1 left well after 23:40—but they can’t both be true.'
    (4) 'I am not guilty; corridor logs place me away at 23:40.'
    (5) '1 was definitely near the artifact at 23:40, contradicting that earlier exit time.'

  Suspect 4:
    (1) '3 was definitely at the display around 23:25, contradicting 3’s denial.'
    (2) 'If 1 is lying, then 2 must be fully correct in everything.'
    (3) 'I am innocent; watchers confirm me elsewhere at 23:50.'
    (4) 'Either 2 hovered by the Phoenix after 23:25, or 3’s entire account is fabricated—but never both.'
    (5) 'If 3 is truthful, then 1’s timeline is completely invalid.'

question: "Which suspect, denoted by \(i \in \{1,2,3,4\}\), is the true thief? Return only the digit."

answer: >
  explanation: "Define \(L(x) = \text{x lies in all five statements},\) \(T(x) = \text{x speaks only truth},\) and \(G(x) = \text{x is guilty}.\)
  We know exactly one \(L(x)=\text{true},\) one \(G(x)=\text{true},\) and the other three \(T(x)=\text{true}.\)

  - Case \(L(1)\): Then suspect 1’s five statements are false ⇒ 1 did not exit before 23:40 ⇒ suspect 1 may still be present at 23:50. Checking times or blame among 2,3,4 as truthful usually triggers conflicts (for instance, suspect 3(5) vs. suspect 1(2)). Contradiction.

  - Case \(L(2)\): Then suspect 2’s five statements are false ⇒ 2 was near the artifact after 23:25 ⇒ suspicious. Meanwhile 1,3,4 remain truthful ⇒ 1(2) stands => 1 not culprit, 3(4) stands => 3 not culprit, 4(3) stands => 4 logs => not culprit ⇒ thus \(G(2)\). No contradictions arise.

  - Case \(L(3)\): Then suspect 3’s five statements are false ⇒ 3 approached after 23:20 ⇒ suspicious. But with 1,2,4 truthful, times mismatch with 4(1), etc. Contradiction.

  - Case \(L(4)\): Then suspect 4’s five statements are false ⇒ 1,2,3 truthful => conflicts with 3(5) or 2(3). Contradiction.

  Thus only \(L(2)\Rightarrow G(2)\) remains consistent. So the culprit is suspect 2.

  final: '2'
"
